sections.paragraph: pr.n. Sennacherib (Herod. Σαναχάριβος), the king of Assyria, from the year 714 to 696 b.c., when he was slain by two of his sons in the temple of Nisroch, 2Ki 18:13 -19:1636 ; Isa 36:1. See also concerning him, Herodotus, ii. 141; and the fragment of Berosus, in Euseb. Chron. Armen. ed. Aucher. t. i. p. 42, 43.
